## Overview
v2.9.17 expands Community Kubernetes support from managed cloud node-pool signals into a local-first Kubernetes scan lane driven by the operator's own `kubectl` context.

## Changes
- Added a Kubernetes scanner module that reads kubeconfig context metadata and scans pods, nodes, persistent volumes, persistent volume claims, services, and workloads through local `kubectl` execution.
- Added Local API scan inputs for `include_kubernetes`, `kubeconfig_path`, and `kube_context` so automation can include Kubernetes evidence without hosted credentials.
- Added Kubernetes Local API endpoints for contexts, scans, findings, nodes, pods, workloads, persistent volumes, persistent volume claims, and services.
- Added Kubernetes governance and action-plan report capability routes for local reporting and MCP-style tool discovery.
- Added kubectl timeout handling, scope fixes for cluster-wide resources, kubeconfig readiness checks, and scan request validation for Kubernetes fields.
- Updated Community wording so the app no longer presents Pro-only or trial-upgrade language in local-first paths.
- Bumped Community app metadata to `2.9.17` for this packaged release.

## Impact
- Community users can run local, read-only Kubernetes/container waste scans when `kubectl` and a read-only kubeconfig context are available.
- Kubernetes findings now join the same local evidence workflow as cloud waste findings, preserving the Community no-hosted-activation boundary.
- The release remains source-visible and production-usable for non-commercial Community use under the repository license.

## Requirements
- `kubectl` must be installed on the operator machine.
- The selected kubeconfig/context must have read-only access to the target cluster.
- Current implementation uses local `kubectl`; direct kube-apiserver client mode remains future work.

## Upgrade/Download
- App metadata version: `2.9.17`
- Community source: `https://github.com/ken-water/cloud-waste-scanner-community`
